title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment · Compiler Case: Response Header Policy
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing layered edge-security plan; use schema-controlled edge policies, layered authorization, observable false positives, private-error cache rules, and bounded degradation states. Planned artifact: Add and verify the response header policy increment in layered edge-security plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Response Header Policy · Theory Response Header Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5999 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Response Header Policy contract for the supplied data.
